instruction: Extract all core computer science concepts from the following lecture JSON. Return only a newline-separated list of unique concepts. Do not explain anything.

text_blocks.text: (Some slides are from Silberschatz, Galvin and Gagne ©2013)

images.ai_description: A plain, solid-colored yellow rectangular prism, representing a closed book or box. The simple design is useful for abstract conceptual presentations.
Illustration of a closed pink book with a light shadow, standing upright. Simple design, emphasizing color contrast and minimalistic style for clarity in presentations.
Blue 3D rectangular prism with a gradient effect. The shape appears solid and is viewed at an angle, showing depth and three distinct sides.
The image features a blue rectangular prism with a lighter blue right face. It illustrates geometric shapes, useful for discussing basic three-dimensional forms.
A plain, solid yellow rectangle with a subtle gradient, suggesting simplicity and minimalism. Perfect as a background or to signify focus on a single topic.
A simple, yellow, rectangular 3D geometric shape with sharp edges and uniform coloring, resembling a tall box or book, set against a plain background.
A tall, rectangular 3D prism with varying shades of pink, viewed from the side, showing depth and dimension. Suitable for geometry or design topics.

This image compares "i-node" and "index block" in file systems, highlighting that they are different methods with distinct terms for data allocation.

images.ai_description: This is an illustration of a green cube, showcasing depth through light and shadow, ideal for discussing geometric shapes and three-dimensional perception in design.

A long, green rectangular prism, possibly a bar or rod, is set against a white background. It illustrates geometric shape and perspective concepts.
A sleek, minimalistic green rectangular box with a bevel effect, casting a subtle shadow. Ideal for highlighting key text or elements on a presentation slide.
A green rectangular prism with clean edges, suspended against a transparent background, illustrating geometric shapes and their spatial orientation for educational purposes.
A simple green rectangular prism representing a three-dimensional shape, illustrating concepts of geometry, volume, and spatial visualization in mathematical and educational contexts.
A three-dimensional green rectangular prism is depicted on a light background, showcasing depth and volume. It illustrates basic geometrical concepts for educational purposes.
A simple blue rectangular prism, illustrating three-dimensional geometric shapes. Useful for teaching basic geometry concepts, spatial understanding, and volume calculations in mathematics education.
A simple, three-dimensional, teal cube with visible dimensions on a white background, illustrating geometric shapes and spatial understanding for educational purposes.
This image shows a simple 3D turquoise rectangle, slightly inclined, with a shadow effect, illustrating basic geometric shapes and color shading for design concepts.
This is a 3D teal rectangular prism, or cuboid, with shaded edges and corners, illustrating geometric shape concepts and spatial perspectives in mathematics.
This image shows a teal, three-dimensional rectangular prism, or cuboid, with a solid appearance and various angles highlighting its geometric form and shadowing.
A purple rectangular prism with a darker top, showcasing depth and dimensionality, symbolizing abstract concepts or data visualization in design and architecture discussions.
A purple rectangular prism is displayed on a white background, illustrating basic three-dimensional geometric shapes for educational purposes in mathematics or engineering lectures.
A long, horizontal, yellow rectangular prism with a shadow, suggesting a 3D effect. The shape is on a plain white background.
A green rectangular prism is depicted, showcasing its length and dimensions. The illustration highlights edges and faces for educational geometry purposes.

images.ai_description: The image illustrates two data structures comparing sequential and linked allocation in file systems, highlighting file attributes, entry lengths, and pointers for memory management.

Illustration of memory management with a buffer showing start, end, and length. Includes a linked list and bitmap for data organization comparison.
Visual comparison of disk block management using linked list and bitmap methods, illustrating free disk blocks and their representation in each technique.
Diagram illustrating free disk space management: showcases linked list and bitmap methods, highlighting disk block allocation using 20-bit and 4-bit representations for optimization.
